ww_detection_rate.long_name: Measles virus detection rate in wastewater surveillance
ww_detection_rate.short_description: Percentage of wastewater samples with measles virus detection
ww_detection_rate.long_description: The detection rate represents the proportion of wastewater samples that tested positive for measles virus RNA across participating wastewater treatment facilities. This metric is calculated by dividing the total number of detections by the total number of samples collected and multiplying by 100. Higher detection rates may indicate increased measles virus circulation in the community served by the wastewater treatment facilities.
ww_detection_rate.statement: In {location}, {value}% of wastewater samples tested positive for measles virus

ww_detection_count.long_name: Total number of measles virus detections in wastewater
ww_detection_count.short_description: Count of wastewater samples with measles virus detected
ww_detection_count.long_description: The total number of wastewater samples that tested positive for measles virus RNA across all participating wastewater treatment facilities in the geographic area. This represents the raw count of detections without adjustment for the number of samples collected or population served.
ww_detection_count.statement: In {location}, measles virus was detected in {value} wastewater samples

ww_sample_count.long_name: Total number of wastewater samples tested for measles
ww_sample_count.short_description: Count of wastewater samples tested for measles virus
ww_sample_count.long_description: The total number of wastewater samples collected and tested for measles virus RNA across all participating wastewater treatment facilities in the geographic area. This represents the denominator used to calculate detection rates.
ww_sample_count.statement: In {location}, {value} wastewater samples were tested for measles virus

ww_population_served.long_name: Total population served by wastewater treatment facilities reporting measles data
ww_population_served.short_description: Number of people served by sewersheds reporting measles surveillance data
ww_population_served.long_description: The total population served by wastewater treatment facilities that submitted measles virus testing data. This represents the coverage of the wastewater surveillance system and indicates the number of people whose wastewater is being monitored for measles virus.
ww_population_served.statement: In {location}, wastewater surveillance for measles covers a population of {value} people

nwss_measles.name: CDC National Wastewater Surveillance System (NWSS) - Measles
nwss_measles.organization: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
nwss_measles.description: The CDC National Wastewater Surveillance System (NWSS) tracks measles virus RNA in wastewater samples from participating wastewater treatment facilities across the United States. Wastewater surveillance provides a complement to traditional clinical surveillance by detecting viral shedding in a community regardless of healthcare-seeking behavior. The measles wastewater surveillance program was expanded in response to the 2025 measles outbreak to provide early warning of community transmission. Data include detection rates (percentage of samples positive), detection counts, sample counts, and population served by participating sewersheds. Surveillance data are aggregated at state and national levels on a weekly basis. This approach can detect measles circulation before cases are clinically confirmed, supporting rapid public health response.
